claim_text: TED defines a calibrated dynamic pathway event object rather than only a gene score, pathway score, or q-value.
supporting_dataset: synthetic benchmark; stress tests; GSE271399; ZSCAPE; C. elegans; osmotic multiome
allowed_wording: TED outputs event-FDR, timing/mode, robustness, artifact checks, identifiability, and claim ceiling as one event object.
forbidden_wording: TED alone proves causal biology or replaces trajectory inference, fate mapping, OT, or pathway scoring.

claim_text: TED remains useful under adversarial noise/dropout/block imbalance because low-identifiability cases are downgraded rather than overclaimed.
supporting_dataset: hard synthetic benchmark; negative-control stress tests; Phase4.6 baseline suite
evidence_type: synthetic benchmark; stress test; baseline comparison
allowed_wording: TED shows lower overclaim and higher artifact rejection than task-specific baseline adapters in the defined TED-object task.
forbidden_wording: TED is superior to every external method on every native task.

claim_text: GSE271399 supports a block-robust GATA1s/T21-context erythroid event-loss mechanism candidate.
evidence_type: family event; block robustness; matched/counterfactual support; external perturbation/multiome support
claim_ceiling: maximum Level 3.5 mechanism candidate; not Level 4
allowed_wording: block-robust, counterfactual-supported, mediation-supported, prioritized erythroid candidate for future functional testing
forbidden_wording: functional causal mechanism; full-length GATA1 rescue proven; clinical disease mechanism proven

claim_text: Within GSE271399, regulatory, maturation, and heme/iron axes transmit part of the erythroid family loss signal without being fully explained by proliferation collapse.
supporting_dataset: GSE214810 driver ablation; Marderstein T21 multiome support
evidence_type: causal mediation proxy; negative mediator control; proliferation-adjusted analysis
allowed_wording: mediation-like support for an upstream regulatory-to-output chain, with negative controls lower than erythroid axes
forbidden_wording: formal biological mediation proven without perturbational rescue of the mediator chain

claim_text: ZSCAPE demonstrates TED-Development event-mode classification across perturbation, time, and embryo blocks.
evidence_type: embryo-block event model; delay/loss classifier; holdout summary
allowed_wording: TED separates developmental delay, true fate loss, state accumulation, fate redirection, and composition artifacts in a perturbation-aware developmental setting.
forbidden_wording: functional rescue or causal mechanism for any specific zebrafish perturbation without targeted validation

claim_text: Arabidopsis osmotic multiome data support an RNA/peak-layer stress-development event candidate, but not yet a full chromatin-first mechanism.
evidence_type: RNA event; ATAC peak event; peak-gene null; gate audit
claim_ceiling: Level 3 RNA/peak-layer event candidate; not Level 3.5 chromatin-first mechanism
allowed_wording: RNA and ATAC/peak-layer osmotic event with null-calibrated peak-gene concordance
forbidden_wording: strong chromatin-first developmental response without motif activity, motif-target concordance, and cell-type-specific lag

claim_text: External proxy datasets repeatedly support GATA1s/GATA1 N-terminus-linked erythroid/heme output weakness, while remaining below rescue-level evidence.
evidence_type: fixed-axis directional concordance; proxy claim ceiling
allowed_wording: multiple adjacent systems directionally support erythroid/heme weakness and selected immature/progenitor features
forbidden_wording: external public data prove GSE271399 rescue, human T21 specificity for all proxies, or Level 4 functional causality

claim_text: GSE298761 adds prospective mouse single-cell support but is capped as non-human, non-T21, non-rescue evidence.
evidence_type: 10x scRNA marker-axis h5ad; QC; sample pseudobulk axis direction
claim_ceiling: mouse GATA1 N-terminus single-cell support; not Level 4
allowed_wording: prospective mouse GATA1s single-cell support for erythroid/heme/maturation down and immature-axis up, with regulatory and housekeeping caveats
forbidden_wording: human T21 rescue, full TED trajectory refit, or functional validation

claim_text: Algorithm sensitivity supports keeping Locked TED-current as primary while promoting ensemble evidence as TED-vNext candidate.
supporting_dataset: GSE271399; ZSCAPE; negative controls; adversarial benchmark
evidence_type: variant registry; stress test; GSE271399 stability; claim ceiling audit
claim_ceiling: algorithm selection decision, not biological evidence upgrade
allowed_wording: Locked TED-current remains the primary reference; EnsembleEvidence is a vNext candidate after raw refit confirmation.
forbidden_wording: algorithm variant selection upgrades biological claims or relaxes Level 4 functional validation requirements

claim_text: Cross-system comparison supports shared dynamic event grammar rather than simple orthologous-gene equivalence.
supporting_dataset: plants; zebrafish; C. elegans; mouse; Drosophila scaffold datasets
evidence_type: event grammar compression; claim ceiling map
allowed_wording: shared event grammar classes such as regulatory priming, delay versus true loss, chromatin-to-RNA lag, and stress-development coupling
forbidden_wording: plant TF A equals animal TF B or conserved causal mechanism without matched perturbation evidence

claim_text: The release does not claim Level 4 functional causality until full-length GATA1 rescue plus targeted RNA/flow/hemoglobinization recovery is completed.
supporting_dataset: GSE271399; external proxy ladder; active wet-lab design
claim_ceiling: maximum Level 3.5 for GSE271399 before rescue
allowed_wording: prioritized candidate for future functional testing
forbidden_wording: functional causal claim, rescue-supported mechanism, or independently replicated causal mechanism

claim_text: GSE157977 provides an independent locked adapter validation of TED claim discipline.
supporting_dataset: locked event families; locked negative controls; locked claim gates
evidence_type: independent adapter validation; guide-level pseudobulk; negative-control gate
claim_ceiling: Level 3 in vivo guide-pseudobulk adapter candidate
allowed_wording: TED identifies a small set of guide-level neural/glial adapter candidates while downgrading or rejecting most control-sensitive guides
forbidden_wording: validated ASD/NDD gene mechanism; cell-type-specific fate loss; developmental delay/loss/redirection; functional causality

claim_text: ClaimCeiling is an evaluable algorithmic output calibrated against a curated evidence-level truth set.
supporting_dataset: GSE271399; ZSCAPE; GSE157977; GSE123013; GSE155254; GSE292039; synthetic benchmark
evidence_type: curated maximum claim level; TED assigned ceiling; overclaim/underclaim audit
claim_ceiling: method-level calibration output; strict Level 4 positive control missing
allowed_wording: TED claim ceilings are evaluated as predictions against curated maximum allowed evidence levels, with overclaim errors explicitly reported.
forbidden_wording: Claim ceilings are only author caution language, or Level 4 is validated without matched functional rescue.

claim_text: TED is compared with direct external method wrappers and a package-complete Docker baseline environment for representative upstream tools.
supporting_dataset: tradeSeq wrapper; GSVA wrapper; AUCell wrapper; POT OT wrapper
evidence_type: direct package execution manifest; Docker baseline environment; TED-object adapter audit
allowed_wording: External packages perform native upstream tasks, while TED adds event mode, artifact gates, block robustness and claim ceiling to construct auditable event objects.
forbidden_wording: TED replaces tradeSeq, GSVA, AUCell, POT, CellRank, CINEMA-OT or WOT on their native tasks.
